task: Launch-quality advanced task: Virtual Production Camera Tracking Drift Reconstruction. Reality context: an LED-volume shoot has camera-tracking drift reported in selected takes. Embedded packet: metric map: metric1=tracking_error_mm, metric2=lens_encoder_error_pct, metric3=timecode_offset_ms, metric4=shot_risk; records: R1 tracking_error_mm=17.85 lens_encoder_error_pct=61.4 timecode_offset_ms=885 shot_risk=788; R2 tracking_error_mm=40.28 lens_encoder_error_pct=53.7 timecode_offset_ms=526 shot_risk=77; R3 tracking_error_mm=11.49 lens_encoder_error_pct=84.7 timecode_offset_ms=168 shot_risk=429; R4 tracking_error_mm=27.72 lens_encoder_error_pct=33.7 timecode_offset_ms=14 shot_risk=29; limit_tracking_error_mm=67.25; limit_lens_encoder_error_pct=29.9; limit_timecode_offset_ms=438; decision_budget_usd=182500; sensitivity_shift_pct=5; hard_threshold_record_count=1; removed_context="SOW required acceptance criteria, traceability, and quantitative evidence". Rules and constraints: Compute priority value = metric1*metric2 - 0.6*metric3 + metric4; rank descending, but any record breaching the hard threshold must be escalated first. Options or recommendations cannot use external measurements, specialized licensed software, or hidden assumptions; all calculations must use only the embedded packet, and any contradictory or under-supported record must be marked needs_review. Required work: reconstruct drift and retake priority; rank records by technical and economic impact, run the stated sensitivity check, and recommend hold, release, retest, or mitigation. Deliver camera_tracking_drift.csv + stage_note.md with formulas or executable logic, record-level citations, validation checks, sensitivity notes, and a final recommendation table suitable for expert review.
